name: Version 7 Post-Classification Parallel Category Routing Runtime
official_runtime_steps: Start one bundled local Ollama server inside the container.
Load official tasks from /input/tasks.json as a top-level array of task_id and prompt records.
Validate task shape, non-empty task IDs and prompts, and unique task IDs.
Classify each task serially with local nemotron-3-nano:4b using a strict canonical category parser.
Resolve the immutable category route by deterministic table lookup.
After all tasks are classified, start answer generation for every routed task.
Run Fireworks-owned answer tasks with bounded concurrency.
Run local Nemotron answer generation serially after the classification barrier.
Use fixed user-message answer wrappers for local answer categories and for FACTUAL_KNOWLEDGE Fireworks answers.
Use the raw official prompt for other primary Fireworks answer categories.
Apply the same category prompt policy to fallback answer calls.
Reconstruct results in original input order.
Atomically write /output/results.json with only task_id and answer fields.
Write optional redacted audit records separately under /output/audit.

scheduler.remote_dispatch: after all classifications finish, bounded by FIREWORKS_MAX_CONCURRENCY
scheduler.local_barrier: all answer generation starts only after all classifications finish
scheduler.local_parallelism: forbidden; one local generation at a time

answer_prompt_policy.wrapper_location: prepended to the single user message; no system prompt is used
answer_prompt_policy.fallback_policy: fallback answer calls use the same category policy and max_completion_tokens as the primary route

fireworks_policy.resolver: exactly one allowed model whose final resource component is kimi-k2p7-code

historical_evidence_policy: Version 5 and Version 6 benchmark and analytics artifacts are retained as historical evidence and are excluded from the Version 7 production image.
